title: Olivia Kaitlyn Roberts
text:
Download CV
Welcome to my website! I am a second year master’s student at the University of Michigan School of Public Health studying Computational Epidemiology and Systems Modeling with a graduate certificate in Complex Systems. My current area of research focuses on tobacco control, where I analyze trends in tobacco use using multistate transition modeling as part of the data analysis and modeling team for the Center for the Assessment of Tobacco Regulations (CAsToR).
I earned my undergraduate degree in Mathematics, with minors in Music and Interdisciplinary Sciences, from the University of South Dakota in 2024. During my time there, I did research on the intersection of mathematics and music, which continues to be an area of interest in my life. I am passionate about teaching mathematics and have experience working as a math tutor, a course assistant for a high school summer math camp, and an instructor assistant for a nursing statistics course.
Outside of academia, I am also a dedicated yoga practitioner and teacher; I have been teaching yoga since 2021 and have organized yoga festivals and workshops. I hold two 200-hour yoga teaching certifications and have experience teaching vinyasa, sculpt, and ashtanga yoga.
